graded_findings.rationale: The gather evidence says this file was updated appropriately with routing entries for the new log sampler design. There is no adherence gap to block on for this ref.
The gather evidence says this file was updated appropriately for the new LogSampler responsibilities and routing. This is not a substantive problem finding.

The gather reports a direct contradiction between the design doc's proto field description and the proto diff. A stale field-number contract in design documentation is a real adherence gap for infrastructure changes.
The gather evidence says this doc removed the obsolete DATASYSTEM_LOG_RATE_LIMIT entry. No actionable documentation gap is identified for this ref.
The gather evidence says this doc now reflects the new sample-rate model and derivation rules. This item is not a blocking or advisory defect.
The gather evidence says this doc replaced log_rate_limit with the new sampling-rate options and semantics. No substantive gap is present in the supplied finding.

The gather reports that core logging design documentation still describes the removed log_rate_limit and LogRateLimiter model. That stale architecture guidance would mislead future work on a hot infrastructure path.

The gather reports that trace-state ownership and sampling behavior remain documented around the old LogRateLimiter model. This is a substantive mismatch with the new request_sample_rate and LogSampler behavior.
The gather reports that the PLOG plan still frames behavior around bypassing the removed log_rate_limit path. Because this affects diagnostic sampling semantics, it should block until corrected.
The gather reports stale implementation-summary text saying PLOG bypasses log_rate_limit request sampling. That is a concrete documentation mismatch after the sampler replacement.

The gather reports that AccessRecorder documentation omits the new facade, lazy materialization, and access_sample_rate pre-drop behavior. This is a material gap for understanding runtime behavior and allocation impact.
